text.title: Offline Toolkit Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `Offline Toolkit Checklist` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The checklist MUST cover a single, dated travel window (e.g. '2026-05-23 → 2026-05-25'). No multi-trip checklists.
rule.rationale: Batching multiple trips into one checklist hides drift across rows; each trip gets its own dated record.

rule.statement: Every checklist row MUST carry a `verify_cmd` (shell-runnable) and an `expected` (string contained in stdout). Free-form prose rows are rejected.
rule.rationale: Untyped rows let operators self-deceive; runnable verify makes the checklist falsifiable.

rule.statement: The checklist names a single accountable owner (the operator) with handle + role. No 'me' or 'us' as owner.
rule.rationale: Future readers need a named human to chase when a row breaks in production CI.

rule.statement: Output carries semver version and `last_reviewed` date within 30 days. Stale checklists (>30 days) MUST be flagged as `needs-refresh` before use.
rule.rationale: Travel-prep changes (new tools, new env vars) decay quickly; forcing refresh prevents using a stale list.

rule.statement: Every verify_cmd MUST be executed and pass with `wifi=off` simulation before the trip starts. Unverified rows are rejected at commit.
rule.rationale: A checklist that hasn't been dry-run on real airplane-mode hardware is a hope, not a checklist.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume cycles and produce false confidence.
